prompt: I'm building an order processing system in Go. Orders are produced by an API and consumed by 3 services: payment, inventory, and notifications. Each service needs to process every order independently. How should I design this with NATS JetStream? Give me the stream config and consumer configs, plus working Go code.

assertions.text: Uses LimitsPolicy or InterestPolicy retention (not WorkQueuePolicy, which destroys messages after one ack — wrong for fanout)
Creates a separate durable consumer for each of the 3 services: payment, inventory, and notifications
Uses AckExplicit (not AckAll or AckNone) for the consumer ack policy
Sets MaxDeliver or mentions dead-letter handling strategy for failed messages
Provides working Go code (not pseudocode) using nats.go — includes actual function calls like nc.Connect, js.AddStream, etc.
Uses a hierarchical subject namespace like 'orders.>' or 'orders.created' (not a flat subject like 'orders')
